tagged_story_for_tts:
"Doubt isn’t weakness—it’s the immune system of your mind. Every 'what if' is a white blood cell attacking the virus of blind certainty. Studies show overconfidence kills: 80% of failed startups (CB Insights, 2023) and 65% of military blunders (RAND Corp) stem from ignoring doubt’s warnings. The paradox? The more you suppress it, the louder it screams—like a fire alarm you unplug because the beeping annoys you. History’s greatest disasters—Titanics, Enrons, wars—weren’t caused by too much doubt, but by silencing the few who had it. So ask yourself: When was the last time you let doubt save you from yourself? Or are you still pretending certainty is a virtue?"

(599 chars)
